title: Tangy Mediterranean Rhubarb Tagine
description: A bitter and gooey Mediterranean dish featuring forced-rhubarb, perfect for a post-workout meal. This satvik-friendly recipe is made with wholesome ingredients and is free from animal products.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay or ceramic tagine over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the forced-rhubarb and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the lemon juice and pomegranate molasses, and stir to combine.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the rhubarb is tender.
Stir in the cooked quinoa and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
